name: General Tsao's Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Rice wine or dry Sherry
2 Dried red chilies, cut in half lengthwise
1 tbsp Coarsely chopped fresh orange peel or
2 tbsp Soaked and coarsely chopped dried citrus peel
1/2 tsp Finely ground roasted Sichuan peppercorns
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken into thin slices, 2 inches long, cutting against the grain.
2. Put chicken in a bowl with soy sauce, rice wine, ginger, cornstarch, and sesame oil.
3. Mix well and let marinate for about 20 minutes.
4. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet until very hot.
5. Remove chicken from marinade using a slotted spoon.
6. Add chicken to the pan and stir-fry for 2 minutes until it browns.
7. Remove chicken and drain in a colander or sieve.
8. Pour off most of the oil, leaving about 2 tbsp.
9. Reheat the pan over high heat and add dried chilies.
10. Stir-fry chilies for 10 seconds, then return chicken to the pan.
11. Add remaining ingredients and stir-fry for 4 minutes, mixing well.
12. Serve immediately over white rice.
